The following is a brief description of the course of sage
Agastya (Canopus), who is an ocean of penance {i.e. spiritual
powers), who purifies the waters, by whom was suppressed the
Vindhya mountain whose peaks grew so high as to obstruct the
Sun's passage, by hom the demon Vatapi who tore the stomachs
of sages was eaten and digested, who drank away the ocean, and
who adorns the southern direction.
[This verse is from the simsTfrctr by our author. This is in the
fliejsrfa'ftfecr metre.]
grRT im 1
sTcsrrte facrg$a<r?ufaar n^n
That sage suddenly made the ocean appear extremely charm-
ing by drying up its waters, in days of yore, through the mountains
(such as the Mainaka which were exposed) situated in its interior
whose peaks were scooped out by the claws of crocodiles (sharks,
whales etc.), and which had streams of water and excellent gems
and jewels falling down along with pearls, as though to out-shine
